Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-06-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Harry L Jacobs; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:11 am and concluding at 9:19 am.

Plaintiff Hua Qiao appearing represented by Woody Wu, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Ying Gu not appearing.

Plaintiff presents summary of facts.

The Court addresses the matter of Venue.

The Court Finds:
1. The Venue is propper and has not been objected to.
2. The conveyance of the property to Defendant Lei Chen was FRAUDULENT.
3. The Deed passing title to Lei Chen is declaired Void.

The Court further ORDERS an Injunction prohibiting any further transfer, conveyance and/or hypothecation of said parcel by any and all Defendants.

The matter is submitted.

By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Hua Qiao, and against Defendant Ying Gu.

Plaintiff's counsel to submit proposed order for review and signature. (D505)

MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 2:25:47 p.m. and concluding at 2:38:41 p.m.

Plaintiff Hua Qiao appearing represented by Woody Wu, Esq., is placed under oath, and testifies via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Ying Gu not appearing.

Counsel for Plaintiff addresses the Court and gives case summary.

The Court questions if San Francisco is the proper venue.

Plaintiff's counsel requests a 60 day continuance to assess the issue of venue.

The Court GRANTS Plaintiff's request for continuance.

The Court orders matter continued to September 6, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505. (D505)
